threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the number 30 bus will actually get you anywhere on time. Welcome to no-man’s land, mate.
Caught the 68 home last night, and a couple was arguing about whether they should move to Brixton. The driver just kept shaking his head.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted the busker playing Bach on a violin in the U-Bahn, while two kids danced around him. That’s when it hit me—this place has soul.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you spot a techno party in a former factory while the U-Bahn shakes the walls. It's a vibe that catches you off guard.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they know to avoid the bus stop at 16th and Mission after 5 PM—absolute chaos, a perfect storm of delays and street performers.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a street musician set up right next to the Muni delay, totally captured the mood. His sax hit a perfect note just as the bus finally rolled in.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about parks and plazas, but it’s the hidden tapas bars in the alleys that show the real local vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still stop for every red light, even when there’s no traffic. True locals know the signal's just a suggestion.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you say 'the Overground' and still think it’s a type of coffee, that’s a dead giveaway. Real locals know it's anything but a caffeine fix.
Caught a midnight 59 last night. Two guys were trying to outdo each other with terrible accents. The bus driver just shook his head, barely amused.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the smell of fresh falafel wafts through the air on a Saturday morning, and you spot the familiar faces at the small market on Hermannstraße.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that doubles as a thrift shop — buy a vintage jacket while sipping on a locally brewed IPA. Pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they can spot the difference between a burrito at La Taqueria and one from Chipotle, even in the dark.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 this morning, and a guy was serenading everyone. When the driver joined in, you could feel the city’s heartbeat. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume parks like Parc Güell are the heart of Gràcia, but the real vibe pulses in the narrow streets of Travessera de Gràcia at dusk.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their struggle to order a simple ‘café con leche’ without mixing up the menus at the bar.
